To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

4/23/2026

City Clerk / Records Custodian
City of Lake Geneva
626 Geneva Street
Lake Geneva, WI 53147

Re: Wisconsin Open Records Law Request — Lake Geneva Business Improvement District Financial Records

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. §§ 19.31–19.39, I hereby request access to and copies of the following public records relating to the Lake Geneva Business Improvement District (BID), including all records held by the City of Lake Geneva in its capacity as the municipality responsible for administering BID special assessments under Wis. Stat. § 66.1109.

CATEGORY 1 — STATUTORY ANNUAL DOCUMENTS
1a. All BID Annual Operating Plans submitted to the Common Council, for all years since BID formation, including any amendments or revisions thereto and the Council resolutions or votes approving, modifying, or rejecting each plan.
1b. All BID Annual Reports prepared pursuant to Wis. Stat. § 66.1109(9), for all available years, including any independent certified audits attached thereto or separately obtained by the City.
1c. Any independent certified audits of BID operating plan implementation obtained by the City under Wis. Stat. § 66.1109(9), for all years for which such audits exist. If no audit was obtained for any year, please so indicate.
CATEGORY 2 — SEGREGATED ACCOUNT & TREASURY RECORDS
2a. All transaction records, ledgers, account statements, and bank statements for the BID segregated municipal treasury account maintained by the City under Wis. Stat. § 66.1109(8), for all available years.
2b. All disbursement authorizations, board orders, or resolutions directing payments from the BID account, for all available years.
2c. Year-end account balance reports or reconciliation records for the BID fund, for all available years.
CATEGORY 3 — SPECIAL ASSESSMENT RECORDS
3a. The complete Special Assessment Roll for the BID for each assessment year, including property addresses, parcel numbers, assessed values, and levy amounts.
3b. All documentation of the assessment methodology used to calculate BID levies, including any changes to that methodology over time.
3c. Records of any delinquent BID assessments and any enforcement actions taken thereon.
CATEGORY 4 — VENDOR CONTRACTS & DISBURSEMENTS
4a. All contracts, agreements, invoices, and payment records for vendors, consultants, or contractors paid from BID funds, for the current fiscal year and the two preceding fiscal years.
4b. Any contracts or funding agreements between the BID and the City of Lake Geneva, VISIT Lake Geneva, or the Lake Geneva Tourism Commission, including any cost-sharing or revenue-sharing arrangements.
4c. All records related to the proposed communications consultant contract discussed at the April 2026 Common Council meeting, including any BID board resolutions authorizing the expenditure, RFP documents, proposals received, and any executed contract.
CATEGORY 5 — BID FORMATION & GOVERNANCE
5a. The original BID creation ordinance and all subsequent amendments.
5b. Current BID board membership roster, including appointment dates and terms.
5c. BID board meeting minutes for the current fiscal year and the two preceding fiscal years.
FEE WAIVER REQUEST

I request a waiver of all fees associated with this request. This request is made on behalf of LakeGenevaNews.com, a news publication covering Lake Geneva municipal government and Walworth County civic affairs. The requested records concern the financial activities of a City-administered special assessment district funded by compulsory levies on property owners. Disclosure of these records will contribute significantly to the public's understanding of how BID funds are collected, managed, and expended. This request is not made for commercial purposes.

Pursuant to Wis. Stat. § 19.35(1)(b), please provide records in electronic format (PDF or spreadsheet) where such format is available, to minimize copying costs.

Wisconsin law requires a response within a reasonable time. I request that you respond within 10 business days and that you notify me promptly if you anticipate any delay, the need to redact any portion of responsive records, or any basis for withholding records, so that I may respond accordingly.

If you have any questions regarding this request, please contact me at the information listed above.

Gary Milliette
LakeGenevaNews.com

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

title: Flock Safety - NCIC

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Flock Safety - NCIC
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

According to your Flock Safety Transparency Portal for automated license plate readers, your agency has listed NCIC among the "Hotlists Alerted On." Within the Flock Safety administrative controls for hotlists, there is an NCIC drop down menu to allow an agency to choose which NCIC "Topics" it will alert on. For example, "Gang or Suspected Terrorist" or "Missing Person."

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: a screen shot of this drop down menu to see which hot lists have been selected/checked off by your agency. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. Please do not hesitate to contact me with any questions at dm@eff.org and 415-436-9333 x151.

Sincerely,

Dave Maass
Director of Investigations
Electronic Frontier Foundation

Dear  Dave  Maass : 

We have received your public records request. This request is being assigned to the appropriate department. The department records access officer will review the request and create an effort estimate to comply with your request where applicable. Pursuant to Massachusetts General Law, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will make a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will begin working on your request. 
If the effort estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that Massachusetts General Law allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. However, the response date may be extended for any of the following reasons: 
1.   Any clarification that is required from you towards this request will stop the clock. The clock will be resumed once the proper clarification is received. 
2.   Payment of estimated effort. The clock stops once the estimate is sent out. It will resume only after the estimated amount has been paid. 
3.   In the records access officer's opinion, if it is determined that the original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort. The due date shall be extended based on statutory regulations. You will be promptly notified in such a case with the revised due date or copied on a petition to the Supervisor of Public Records.

title: NV CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (White Pine County Clerk/Registrar of Voters)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: NV CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (White Pine County Clerk/Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections: 
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election 
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in White Pine County in that year.

In case it is helpful, you can find an example of the records I am requesting on the Clark County webpage, where they have made these records publicly available for the 2024 General Election. You can see this by navigating to their Election Information page, then clicking to the “Reports, Data, Maps” Section. If you scroll down, you will see “2024 Election Information” and then an itemized list that includes the item “Cast Vote Record for the Nov 5, 2024, General Election”, which is a link that downloads the data directly. I paste the link to the webpage here: https://www.clarkcountynv.gov/government/departments/elections/reports_data_maps/

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska
